evidence_quotes:
Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: string

/api/loans/{id}/return:
    post:
      ...
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Loan returned
        '409':
          description: Loan already returned
router.post('/api/loans/:id/return', ...) { ... if (err instanceof Error && err.message === 'Loan not found') { res.status(404).json({ error: err.message });
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 (Well-designed) does not apply: error responses contain only a human message — no stable `code` field for programmatic handling (handlers do `res.status(409).json({ error: err.message })`); the OpenAPI provides no examples for negative ACs; domain-error→HTTP mapping is duplicated in each handler via instanceof checks rather than centralized in a single middleware; and response schemas have mismatches with implementation (Error schema declares `error: string` but validation errors actually return `error: array of zod issues`; POST endpoints like /api/books, /api/members, /api/loans, /api/reservations omit the 400 response in the spec; POST /api/loans/{id}/return omits 404 in the spec despite the handler returning it).
